description: Worktree false-positive fix: path normalization prevents misclassification when git-common-dir and git-dir differ by format (relative vs absolute) but resolve to the same directory

problem.symptom: isWorktree reported true in a normal (non-worktree) checkout when invoked from a subdirectory
problem.root_cause: Raw string inequality between git-common-dir output (e.g., '.git') and git-dir output (e.g., '/home/user/project/.git') — same directory, different string representations

fix.approach: Added normalizeGitPath() that resolves both paths to absolute form via path.resolve(base, raw), then applies fs.realpathSync with fallback to path.normalize for offline/test environments

new_tests.name: AT-S19-01: no false worktree when relative and absolute paths resolve to the same .git
new_tests.scenario: git-common-dir returns '.git', git-dir returns '/home/user/project/.git', toplevel is '/home/user/project'

new_tests.name: normalizeGitPath: resolves relative path against base

preserved_tests.name: AT-S19-01: detects worktree when git-common-dir differs from git-dir (after normalization)
detects non-worktree when git-common-dir equals git-dir (both relative)
